ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Ka Yisahaku daa chɛ ka Yaakubu kuli Mɛsopoteemia Leeban ŋun daa nyɛ Bɛtuɛl Aram nir’ bia, ka nyɛ Yaakubu mini Iso ma Ribɛka tizɔdoo la sani. Iso lan kpuɣi paɣ’ so
translated: And Isaac called Jacob and blessed him and charged him, saying, "You shall not marry one of the Canaanite women. Arise, go to Paddan-aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother's father, and take as your wife from there one of the daughters of Laban your mother's brother."

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: amaa Naawuni yɛl’ ti, ‘Miriya ka yi di ti’ shɛli din be puu ŋɔ sunsuuni ŋɔ wala bee n-shihi li gba, yi yi niŋ lala, yi ni kpi.’ ”
translated: but God said, 'You shall not eat of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, nor shall you touch it, lest you die.'

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Ka Haga daa boli Yawɛ ŋun tɔɣis’ o yɛtɔɣa maa yuli, “Naawuni ŋun nyara;” dama o daa yɛlimi, “Ka daɣila n shiri nyala Naawuni ka na be n nyɛvil’ ni n ni nya o maa nyaaŋa?”
translated: And the Prophet said, "God is the one who knows"; because he said, "How can I hide from God when I am in His presence and He sees me?"

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: N ni chɛ ka m min’ a lo tab’ alikauli, ka n chɛ ka a zuliya nab’ pam.”
translated: I want you to know that I love you, and I want you to love me too.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: A ni nya tiŋgbɔn shɛli maa zaa, n ni zaŋ li ti a zuliya ka bɛ su li sahakam.
translated: And that town, I will take it to my village and they will make it a permanent place
